title: Conjecture 4
statement_text: A monotone class of graphs $\mathcal{C}$ has bounded expansion if and only if for every fixed $h \geq 1$, posets of height at most $h$ whose cover graphs are in $\mathcal{C}$ have bounded dimension.
context_text: This conjecture from reference [12] asserts that bounded expansion exactly characterises the graph classes for which poset dimension is bounded purely as a function of height. The forward direction was established in [12] and is central motivation for the present paper's approach via weak coloring numbers.
paper_contribution: Reproves the forward direction (bounded expansion implies $O(n^\varepsilon)$-dimension for fixed height) via weak coloring numbers with a simpler proof and quantitatively better bounds (Theorem 2); explicitly notes the backward direction remains open.
notes: Full bibliography not present in extracted text; [12] is cited as the source of both the proved forward direction and the open backward direction. attributed_year set to null.
paper_title: Nowhere Dense Graph Classes and Dimension
